function printUsage(): void { 153: const msg = ` 154: Usage: 155: node --strip-types scripts/categorize-magic-numbers.ts [options] 156: Options: 157: --eslint-json <path> Use an existing ESLint JSON report. 158: If omitted, runs: ./node_modules/.bin/eslint . -f json 159: --out <path> Output JSON path (default: docs/magic-number-categories.json) 160: --context <n> Context lines per occurrence (default: 2) 161: --max-candidates <n> Top constant matches per literal group (default: 5) 162: --min-semantic-score <0..1> Minimum semantic score to keep candidate (default: 0.2) 163: runCommand(command: string, args: string[], cwd: string): string { 169: const result = spawnSync(command, args, { cwd, encoding: 'utf8' }); 170: if (result.error) { 171: throw result.error; 172: } 173: if (result.status !== 0) { 174: throw new Error(`${command} ${args.join(' ')} failed with status ${result.status}
${result.stderr}`); 175: } 176: return result.stdout; 177: } 178: function loadEslintResults(options: CliOptions): EslintResult[] { 179: if (options.eslintJson) { 180: const raw = fs.readFileSync(options.eslintJson, 'utf8'); 181: return JSON.parse(raw) as EslintResult[]; 182: } 183: const stdout = runCommand('./node_modules/.bin/eslint', ['.', '-f', 'json'], process.cwd()); 184: return JSON.parse(stdout) as EslintResult[]; 185: } 186: function parseMessageLiteral(message: string): string | null { 187: const match = /No magic number:\s*([^\s]+)\.?/u.exec(message); 188: if (!match) return null; 189: return match[1].trim(); 190: } 191: function parseNumericLiteral(literal: string): number | null { 192: let candidate = literal.trim(); 193: candidate = candidate.replace(/[,;:]$/u, ''); 194: candidate = candidate.replace(/n$/u, ''); 195: if (candidate.endsWith('.') && candidate !== '.') candidate = candidate.slice(0, -1); 196: candidate = candidate.replace(/_/g, ''); 197: if (!candidate) return null; 198: const value = Number(candidate); 199: return Number.isFinite(value) ? 0 : intersection / union; 233: } 234: function normalizeLiteralForGrouping(literal: string): string { 235: return literal.trim(); 236: } 237: function gatherOccurrences(results: EslintResult[], contextLines: number): MagicOccurrence[] { 238: const contentCache = new Map<string, string[]>(); 239: const occurrences: MagicOccurrence[] = []; 240: let idCounter = 0; 241: for (const result of results) { 242: const filePath = path.resolve(result.filePath); 243: const relativePath = path.relative(process.cwd(), filePath); 244: let lines = contentCache.get(filePath); 245: if (!lines) { 246: if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) continue; 247: lines = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8').split(/?
/u); 248: contentCache.set(filePath, lines); 249: } 250: for (const message of result.messages) { 251: if (message.ruleId !== 'no-magic-numbers') continue; 252: const literalRaw = parseMessageLiteral(message.message); 253: if (!literalRaw) continue; 254: const numeric = parseNumericLiteral(literalRaw); 255: if (numeric === null) continue; 256: const line = message.line ?? 1; 257: const column = message.column ?? 1; 258: const lineText = lines[line - 1] ?? ''; 259: const start = Math.max(1, line - contextLines); 260: const end = Math.min(lines.length, line + contextLines); 261: const context: string[] = []; 262: for (let ln = start; ln <= end; ln += 1) { 263: context.push(`${ln}: ${lines[ln - 1] ?? ''}`); 264: } 265: const tokens = buildTokenSet(relativePath, lineText, context.join('
')); 266: occurrences.push({ 267: id: `m${idCounter++}`, 268: filePath, 269: relativePath, 270: line, 271: column, 272: literal: normalizeLiteralForGrouping(literalRaw), 273: numericValue: numeric, 274: message: message.message, 275: snippet: lineText.trim(), 276: context, 277: tokens, 278: }); 279: } 280: } 281: return occurrences; 282: } 283: function listConstantFiles(): string[] { 284: const stdout = runCommand('rg', [ 285: '--files', 286: '-g', '*constants*.ts', 287: '-g', '*constants*.py', 288: '-g', '*units.ts', 289: '-g', '*score-thresholds.ts', 290: '-g', '*http-status.ts', 291: '-g', '!docs/repomix/**', 292: '-g', '!node_modules/**', 293: ], process.cwd()); 294: return stdout 295: .split('
') 296: .map((line) => line.trim()) 297: .filter(Boolean) 298: .map((rel) => path.resolve(process.cwd(), rel)); 299: } 300: function extractConstantsFromFile(filePath: string): ConstantDef[] { 301: const relativePath = path.relative(process.cwd(), filePath); 302: const lines = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8').split(/?
/u); 303: const constants: ConstantDef[] = []; 304: let idCounter = 0; 305: const numberPattern = '(-?(?:0x[0-9A-Fa-f_]+|0o[0-7_]+|0b[01_]+|(?:\d[\d_]*\.?\d*|\.\d+)(?:e[+-]?\d+)?n?))'; 306: const regexes: RegExp[] = [ 307: new RegExp(`^\s*(?:export\s+)?(?:const|let|var)\s+([A-Z][A-Z0-9_]+)\s*=\s*${numberPattern}`), 308: new RegExp(`^\s*([A-Z][A-Z0-9_]+)\s*:\s*${numberPattern}`), 309: new RegExp(`^\s*([A-Z][A-Z0-9_]+)\s*=\s*${numberPattern}`), 310: ]; 311: for (let index = 0; index < lines.length; index += 1) { 312: const line = lines[index]; 313: const trimmed = line.trim(); 314: if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('//') || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue; 315: for (const re of regexes) { 316: const match = re.exec(line); 317: if (!match) continue; 318: const [, name, literal] = match; 319: const numericValue = parseNumericLiteral(literal); 320: if (numericValue === null) continue; 321: const tokens = buildTokenSet(name, relativePath, line); 322: constants.push({ 323: id: `c${path.basename(filePath)}_${idCounter++}`, 324: name, 325: filePath, 326: relativePath, 327: line: index + 1, 328: literal, 329: numericValue, 330: snippet: trimmed, 331: tokens, 332: }); 333: break; 334: } 335: } 336: return constants; 337: } 338: function gatherConstants(): ConstantDef[] { 339: const files = listConstantFiles(); 340: const constants: ConstantDef[] = []; 341: for (const filePath of files) { 342: constants.push(...extractConstantsFromFile(filePath)); 343: } 344: const seen = new Set<string>(); 345: const deduped: ConstantDef[] = []; 346: for (const constant of constants) { 347: const key = `${constant.relativePath}:${constant.line}:${constant.name}:${constant.numericValue}`; 348: if (seen.has(key)) continue; 349: seen.add(key); 350: deduped.push(constant); 351: } 352: return deduped; 353: } 354: function numbersEqual(a: number, b: number): boolean { 355: return Math.abs(a - b) < 1e-9; 356: } 357: function scoreCandidate( 358: literalValue: number, 359: groupTokens: Set<string>, 360: constant: ConstantDef 361: ): ConstantCandidate { 362: const valueMatch = numbersEqual(literalValue, constant.numericValue); 363: const semanticScore = jaccardSimilarity(groupTokens, constant.tokens); 364: return { 365: constantId: constant.id, 366: name: constant.name, 367: path: constant.relativePath, 368: line: constant.line, 369: literal: constant.literal, 370: numericValue: constant.numericValue, 371: semanticScore, 372: valueMatch, 373: }; 374: } 375: class UnionFind { 376: private parent: number[]; 377: private rank: number[]; 378: constructor(size: number) { 379: this.parent = Array.from({ length: size }, (_, i) => i); 380: this.rank = new Array(size).fill(0); 381: } 382: find(x: number): number { 383: if (this.parent[x] !== x) { 384: this.parent[x] = this.find(this.parent[x]); 385: } 386: return this.parent[x]; 387: } 388: union(a: number, b: number): void { 389: const rootA = this.find(a); 390: const rootB = this.find(b); 391: if (rootA === rootB) return; 392: if (this.rank[rootA] < this.rank[rootB]) { 393: this.parent[rootA] = rootB; 394: } else if (this.rank[rootA] > this.rank[rootB]) { 395: this.parent[rootB] = rootA; 396: } else { 397: this.parent[rootB] = rootA; 398: this.rank[rootA] += 1; 399: } 400: } 401: } 402: function clusterPeers(occurrences: MagicOccurrence[], threshold: number): PeerClusterSummary[] { 403: if (occurrences.length === 0) return []; 404: const uf = new UnionFind(occurrences.length); 405: for (let i = 0; i < occurrences.length; i += 1) { 406: for (let j = i + 1; j < occurrences.length; j += 1) { 407: const similarity = jaccardSimilarity(occurrences[i].tokens, occurrences[j].tokens); 408: if (similarity >= threshold) { 409: uf.union(i, j); 410: } 411: } 412: } 413: const buckets = new Map<number, MagicOccurrence[]>(); 414: for (let i = 0; i < occurrences.length; i += 1) { 415: const root = uf.find(i); 416: const arr = buckets.get(root) ?? []; 417: arr.push(occurrences[i]); 418: buckets.set(root, arr); 419: } 420: let clusterIdx = 0; 421: const clusters: PeerClusterSummary[] = []; 422: for (const members of buckets.values()) { 423: const tokenFreq = new Map<string, number>(); 424: for (const member of members) { 425: for (const token of member.tokens) { 426: tokenFreq.set(token, (tokenFreq.get(token) ?? 0) + 1); 427: } 428: } 429: const minFreq = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(members.length * 0.6)); 430: const sharedTokens = Array.from(tokenFreq.entries()) 431: .filter(([, freq]) => freq >= minFreq) 432: .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]) 433: .slice(0, 10) 434: .map(([token]) => token); 435: clusters.push({ 436: clusterId: `cluster-${clusterIdx++}`, 437: size: members.length, 438: sharedTokens, 439: sampleOccurrences: members.slice(0, 5).map((o) => ({ 440: path: o.relativePath, 441: line: o.line, 442: column: o.column, 443: snippet: o.snippet, 444: })), 445: }); 446: } 447: clusters.sort((a, b) => b.size - a.size); 448: return clusters; 449: } 450: function summarizeGroups( 451: occurrences: MagicOccurrence[], 452: constants: ConstantDef[], 453: options: CliOptions 454: ): LiteralGroupSummary[] { 455: const byLiteral = new Map<string, MagicOccurrence[]>(); 456: for (const occurrence of occurrences) { 457: const arr = byLiteral.get(occurrence.literal) ?? []; 458: arr.push(occurrence); 459: byLiteral.set(occurrence.literal, arr); 460: } 461: const groups: LiteralGroupSummary[] = []; 462: for (const [literal, items] of byLiteral.entries()) { 463: const numericValue = items[0].numericValue; 464: const tokenFreq = new Map<string, number>(); 465: for (const item of items) { 466: for (const token of item.tokens) { 467: tokenFreq.set(token, (tokenFreq.get(token) ?? 0) + 1); 468: } 469: } 470: const topTokens = Array.from(tokenFreq.entries()) 471: .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]) 472: .slice(0, 30) 473: .map(([token]) => token); 474: const groupTokens = new Set(topTokens); 475: const scored = constants 476: .map((constant) => scoreCandidate(numericValue, groupTokens, constant)) 477: .sort((a, b) => { 478: if (b.valueMatch !== a.valueMatch) return Number(b.valueMatch) - Number(a.valueMatch); 479: return b.semanticScore - a.semanticScore; 480: }); 481: const exactMatches = scored 482: .filter((candidate) => candidate.valueMatch) 483: .slice(0, options.maxCandidates); 484: const semanticMatches = scored 485: .filter((candidate) => candidate.semanticScore >= options.minSemanticScore) 486: .slice(0, options.maxCandidates); 502: } 503: groups.sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count); 504: return groups; 505: } 506: function toSerializableReport(report: SummaryReport): JsonValue { 507: return report as unknown as JsonValue; 508: } 509: function main(): void { 510: const options = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2)); 134: const archiveName = `${path.basename(log.name, '.json')}-${timestamp}.json.gz`; 135: const archivePath = path.join(archiveDir, archiveName); 136: if (dryRun) { 137: if (verbose) { 138: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would archive: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 139: } 140: } else { 141: try { 142: await compressFile(log.path, archivePath); 143: await fs.unlink(log.path); 144: if (verbose) { 145: console.log(` ✓ Archived: ${log.name} → ${archiveName}`); 146: } 147: archivedCount++; 148: totalSize += log.sizeBytes; 149: } catch (error) { 150: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to archive error log', { file: log.name }); 151: } 152: } 153: } 154: const action = dryRun ? '[DRY RUN] Would archive' : 'Archived'; 155: console.log(`${action} ${toArchive.length} error logs (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 156: return { archived: archivedCount, totalSize }; 157: } 158: async function deleteOldArchives(archivedLogs, retentionDays, dryRun, verbose) { 159: const toDelete = archivedLogs.filter(log => log.ageDays > retentionDays); 160: if (toDelete.length === 0) { 161: console.log('✓ No old archives need deletion'); 162: return { deleted: 0, totalSize: 0 }; 163: } 164: let deletedCount = 0; 165: let totalSize = 0; 166: for (const log of toDelete) { 167: if (dryRun) { 168: if (verbose) { 169: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would delete: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 170: } 171: } else { 172: try { 173: await fs.unlink(log.path); 174: if (verbose) { 175: console.log(` ✓ Deleted: ${log.name}`); 176: } 177: deletedCount++; 178: totalSize += log.sizeBytes; 179: } catch (error) { 180: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to delete archived log', { file: log.name }); 181: } 182: } 183: } 184: const action = dryRun ? '[DRY RUN] Would delete' : 'Deleted'; 185: console.log(`${action} ${toDelete.length} old archives (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 186: return { deleted: deletedCount, totalSize }; 187: } 188: function printCleanupSummary(archiveResult: { archived: number; totalSize: number }, deleteResult: { deleted: number; totalSize: number }, dryRun: boolean) { 189: console.log('
' + '='.repeat(50)); 190: console.log('📊 Summary'); 191: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 192: console.log(`Error logs archived: ${archiveResult.archived}`); 193: console.log(`Old archives deleted: ${deleteResult.deleted}`); 194: console.log(`Total space reclaimed: ${formatBytes(archiveResult.totalSize + deleteResult.totalSize)}`); 195: if (dryRun) console.log('
⚠️ This was a dry run - no files were modified'); 196: } 197: async function cleanup(options) { 198: console.log('

'); 199: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 200: console.log(`Retention period: ${options.retentionDays} days`); 201: console.log(`Archive retention: ${ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS} days`); 202: console.log(`Mode: ${options.dryRun ? 'DRY RUN (no changes)' : 'LIVE'}`); 203: console.log('='.repeat(50) + '
'); 204: console.log('📂 Scanning for error logs...'); 205: const errorLogs = await scanErrorLogs(LOGS_BASE_DIR); 206: console.log(`Found ${errorLogs.length} error log files

Total: {len(violations)} functions need refactoring") 69: if __name__ == "__main__": 70: main() 1: import argparse 2: import re 3: import shutil 4: from dataclasses import dataclass 5: from pathlib import Path 6: from typing import Dict, List, Tuple 7: from ast_grep_mcp.constants import FormattingDefaults, SemanticVolumeDefaults 8: from ast_grep_mcp.utils.console_logger import console 9: try: 10: from scripts.import_helpers import CONSOLE_IMPORT_STMT, compute_import_insert_index, ensure_import_present, scan_import_state 11: except ImportError: 12: from import_helpers import CONSOLE_IMPORT_STMT, compute_import_insert_index, ensure_import_present, scan_import_state 13: @dataclass 14: class PrintStatement: 15: file_path: str 16: line_number: int 17: column: int 18: original_code: str 19: suggested_replacement: str 20: print_type: str 21: class PrintMigrator: 22: def __init__(self, dry_run: bool = True, backup: bool = False): 23: self.dry_run = dry_run 24: self.backup = backup 25: self.import_statement = CONSOLE_IMPORT_STMT 26: def analyze_print_call(self, code: str) -> str: 27: if "json.dumps" in code or "asdict" in code: 28: return "json" 29: if re.search(r'print\(["']([=\-_*])\1+["']\)', code): 30: return "separator" 31: # Check for error output ( or 'error' in string) 32: if "" in code or "error" in code.lower(): 33: return "error" 34: if code.startswith('console.log(f"') or code.startswith("console.log(f'"): 35: return "formatted" 36: return "simple" 37: def migrate_print_statement(self, code: str, print_type: str) -> str: 38: match = re.match(r"print\((.*)\)$", code.strip(), re.DOTALL) 39: if not match: 40: # Fallback: return original if we can't parse 41: return code 42: content = match.group(1) 43: # Remove if present 44: content = re.sub(r",?\s*file=sys\.stderr", "", content) 45: # Handle different types 46: if print_type == "json": 47: if "json.dumps" in content: 48: json_match = re.search(r"json\.dumps\((.*?)(,\s*indent=\d+)?\)", content) 49: if json_match: 50: data = json_match.group(1) 51: return f"console.json({data})" 52: return f"console.log({content})" 53: elif print_type == "separator": 54: sep_match = re.search(r'["']([=\-_*])\1*["']', content) 55: if sep_match: 56: char = sep_match.group(1) 57: length = len(re.search(r'["']([=\-_*]+)["']', content).group(1)) 58: return f'console.separator("{char}", {length})' 59: return f"console.log({content})" 60: elif print_type == "error": 61: return f"console.error({content})" 62: elif print_type == "formatted": 63: return f"console.log({content})" 64: else: # simple 65: return f"console.log({content})" 66: def find_print_statements(self, file_path: Path) -> List[PrintStatement]: 67: """Find all console.blank() statements in a file. 68: Args: 69: file_path: Path to Python file 70: Returns: 71: List of PrintStatement objects 72: """ 73: statements = [] 74: try: 75: with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f: 76: content = f.read() 77: lines = content.split("
") 78: # Use regex to find print statements (simple approach) 79: for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1): 80: # Skip comments 81: if line.strip().startswith("#"): 82: continue 83: # Find console.log( statements 84: for match in re.finditer(r" print\s*\(", line): 85: # Extract full print statement (may span multiple lines) 86: start_col = match.start() 87: original_code = self._extract_print_call(lines, line_num - 1, start_col) 88: if original_code: 89: print_type = self.analyze_print_call(original_code) 90: replacement = self.migrate_print_statement(original_code, print_type) 91: statements.append( 92: PrintStatement( 93: file_path=str(file_path), 94: line_number=line_num, 95: column=start_col, 96: original_code=original_code, 97: suggested_replacement=replacement, 98: print_type=print_type, 99: ) 100: ) 101: except Exception as e: 102: console.log(f"Error analyzing {file_path}: {e}") 103: return statements 104: def _extract_print_call(self, lines: List[str], start_line: int, start_col: int) -> str: 105: """Extract complete console.blank() call that may span multiple lines. 106: Args: 107: lines: All lines in the file 108: start_line: Line number where console.log( starts (0-indexed) 109: start_col: Column where console.log( starts 110: Returns: 111: Complete console.blank() statement as string 112: """ 113: # Simple extraction - find matching closing parenthesis 114: line = lines[start_line][start_col:] 115: depth = 0 116: in_string = False 117: string_char = None 118: escaped = False 119: result = [] 120: for _i, char in enumerate(line): 121: if escaped: 122: escaped = False 123: result.append(char) 124: continue 125: if char == "\": 126: escaped = True 127: result.append(char) 128: continue 129: if char in ('"', "'") and not in_string: 130: in_string = True 131: string_char = char 132: result.append(char) 133: elif char == string_char and in_string: 134: in_string = False 135: string_char = None 136: result.append(char) 137: elif char == "(" and not in_string: 138: depth += 1 139: result.append(char) 140: elif char == ")" and not in_string: 141: depth -= 1 142: result.append(char) 143: if depth == 0: 144: return "".join(result) 145: else: 146: result.append(char) 147: # If we didn't find closing paren on same line, it might span multiple lines 148: return "".join(result) if result else None 149: def _read_file_lines(self, file_path: Path) -> List[str]: 150: """Read file content and return split lines.""" 151: with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as file_obj: 152: return file_obj.read().split("
") 153: def _create_backup_if_needed(self, file_path: Path, changes: List[str]) -> None: 154: if self.backup and not self.dry_run: 155: backup_path = file_path.with_suffix(".py.bak") 156: shutil.copy(file_path, backup_path) 157: changes.append(f"Created backup: {backup_path}") 158: def _scan_import_state(self, lines: List[str]) -> Tuple[bool, int]: 159: """Return whether console import exists and last import line index.""" 160: return scan_import_state(lines, self.import_statement) 161: def _apply_statement_replacements(self, lines: List[str], statements: List[PrintStatement], changes: List[str]) -> None: 162: """Replace print statements in reverse order to preserve line indices.""" 163: for stmt in reversed(statements): 164: line_idx = stmt.line_number - 1 165: line = lines[line_idx] 166: lines[line_idx] = line.replace(stmt.original_code, stmt.suggested_replacement) 167: changes.append(f"Line {stmt.line_number}: {stmt.original_code.strip()} -> {stmt.suggested_replacement.strip()}") 168: def _compute_import_insert_index(self, lines: List[str]) -> int: 169: """Find insertion index after shebang/docstring for import insertion.""" 170: return compute_import_insert_index(lines) 171: def _ensure_import_present(self, lines: List[str], has_import: bool, import_line_index: int, changes: List[str]) -> None: 172: """Ensure console import exists in file content.""" 173: if has_import: 174: return 175: _ = import_line_index # Kept for compatibility with current call signature. 176: if ensure_import_present(lines, self.import_statement, add_blank_line=True, blank_line_only_when_needed=False): 177: changes.append(f"Added import: {self.import_statement}") 178: def _write_lines_if_needed(self, file_path: Path, lines: List[str]) -> None: 179: """Persist updated lines unless running in dry-run mode.""" 180: if self.dry_run: 181: return 182: with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as file_obj: 183: file_obj.write("
".join(lines)) 184: def migrate_file(self, file_path: Path) -> Tuple[int, List[str]]: 185: statements = self.find_print_statements(file_path) 186: if not statements: 187: return 0, [] 188: changes = self._apply_statement_replacements(lines, statements, changes) 193: self._ensure_import_present(lines, has_import, import_line_index, changes) 194: self._write_lines_if_needed(file_path, lines) 195: 24: return file_obj.readlines() 25: def write_lines(file_path: Path, lines: Sequence[str]) -> None: 26: with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as file_obj: 27: file_obj.writelines(lines) 28: def _normalize_ranges(ranges: Sequence[Tuple[int, int]]) -> List[Tuple[int, int]]: 29: if not ranges: 30: return [] 31: valid = sorted((start, end) for start, end in ranges if start < end) 32: if not valid: 33: return [] 34: merged: List[Tuple[int, int]] = [valid[0]] 35: for start, end in valid[1:]: 36: prev_start, prev_end = merged[-1] 37: if start <= prev_end: 38: merged[-1] = (prev_start, max(prev_end, end)) 39: else: 40: merged.append((start, end)) 41: return merged 42: def remove_line_ranges(lines: Sequence[str], ranges: Sequence[Tuple[int, int]]) -> List[str]: 43: merged = _normalize_ranges(ranges) 44: if not merged: 45: return list(lines) 46: result: List[str] = [] 47: range_idx = 0 48: current_start, current_end = merged[range_idx] 49: for line_idx, line in enumerate(lines): 50: while range_idx < len(merged) and line_idx >= current_end: 51: range_idx += 1 52: if range_idx < len(merged): 53: current_start, current_end = merged[range_idx] 54: in_range = range_idx < len(merged) and current_start <= line_idx < current_end 55: if not in_range: 56: result.append(line) 57: return result 1: # Scripts 2: 3: This directory contains utility scripts for running ast-grep MCP tools from the command line. 4: 5: ## find_duplication.py 6: 7: Python script to detect duplicate code in a project. 8: 9: ### Usage 10: 11: ```bash 12: # Basic usage - analyze Python functions 13: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/project --language python 14: 15: # Analyze JavaScript classes 16: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/project --language javascript --construct-type class_definition 17: 18: # Strict similarity threshold (90%) 19: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/project --language python --min-similarity 0.9 20: 21: # Ignore small functions (less than 10 lines) 22: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/project --language python --min-lines 10 23: 24: # Output as JSON 25: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/project --language python --json 26: ``` 27: 28: ### Options 29: 30: - `project_folder` (required): Absolute path to the project to analyze 31: - `--language, -l` (required): Programming language (python, javascript, typescript, java, go, etc.) 32: - `--construct-type, -c`: Type of construct to analyze (default: function_definition) 33: - `function_definition`: Regular functions 34: - `class_definition`: Classes 35: - `method_definition`: Methods within classes 36: - `--min-similarity, -s`: Minimum similarity threshold 0.0-1.0 (default: 0.8) 37: - `--min-lines, -m`: Minimum lines to consider for duplication (default: 5) 38: - `--json, -j`: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text 39: 40: ### Examples 41: 42: **Find duplicate Python functions:** 43: ```bash 44: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/my-project --language python 45: ``` 46: 47: **Find duplicate JavaScript classes with strict matching:** 48: ```bash 49: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/my-app \ 50: --language javascript \ 51: --construct-type class_definition \ 52: --min-similarity 0.95 53: ``` 54: 55: **Analyze only larger functions (10+ lines):** 56: ```bash 57: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/my-project \ 58: --language python \ 59: --min-lines 10 60: ``` 61: 62: **Get JSON output for programmatic processing:** 63: ```bash 64: uv run python scripts/find_duplication.py /path/to/my-project \ 65: --language python \ 66: --json > duplication-report.json 67: ``` 68: 69: ## find_duplication.sh 70: 71: Bash wrapper for the Python script (simpler interface). 72: 73: ### Usage 74: 75: ```bash 76: # Basic usage 77: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /path/to/project python 78: 79: # With construct type 80: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /path/to/project javascript class_definition 81: 82: # With similarity threshold 83: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /path/to/project python function_definition 0.9 84: 85: # With all options 86: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /path/to/project python function_definition 0.8 10 87: ``` 88: 89: ### Arguments (positional) 90: 91: 1. `project_folder` (required): Absolute path to the project 92: 2. `language` (optional, default: python): Programming language 93: 3. `construct_type` (optional, default: function_definition): Type of construct 94: 4. `min_similarity` (optional, default: 0.8): Similarity threshold 95: 5. `min_lines` (optional, default: 5): Minimum lines 96: 97: ### Examples 98: 99: **Analyze Python project:** 100: ```bash 101: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /Users/me/myproject python 102: ``` 103: 104: **Analyze JavaScript classes:** 105: ```bash 106: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /Users/me/webapp javascript class_definition 107: ``` 108: 109: **Strict matching (95% similarity):** 110: ```bash 111: ./scripts/find_duplication.sh /Users/me/myproject python function_definition 0.95 112: ``` 113: 114: ## Output Format 115: 116: The script outputs a formatted report with three sections: 117: 118: ### 1. Summary 119: - Total constructs analyzed 120: - Duplicate groups found 121: - Total duplicated lines 122: - Potential line savings 123: - Analysis time 124: 125: ### 2. Duplication Groups 126: - Group ID and similarity score 127: - List of duplicate instances with file locations 128: - Code preview for each instance 129: 130: ### 3. Refactoring Suggestions 131: - Suggestion type (Extract Function, Base Class, etc.) 132: - Description and recommendation 133: - Number of duplicates and lines 134: - File locations for all duplicates 135: 136: ## Supported Languages 137: 138: The tool works with all languages supported by ast-grep: 139: 140: - JavaScript/TypeScript 141: - Python 142: - Java 143: - Go 144: - Rust 145: - C/C++ 146: - C# 147: - Ruby 148: - PHP 149: - And many more... 150: 151: See the [ast-grep language documentation](https://ast-grep.github.io/reference/languages.html) for a complete list. 152: 153: ## Tips 154: 155: 1. **Start with defaults**: Try running with default settings first to get a baseline 156: 2. **Adjust similarity**: Lower threshold (0.7) finds more potential duplicates; higher (0.9) is more strict 157: 3. **Filter small code**: Use `--min-lines 10` to focus on larger duplications 158: 4. **JSON output**: Use `--json` for integration with other tools or CI/CD pipelines 159: 5. **Large projects**: The analysis may take longer on large codebases; be patient 160: 161: ## Troubleshooting 162: 163: **Error: "Command not found: ast-grep"** 164: - Install ast-grep: `brew install ast-grep` or see [installation guide](https://ast-grep.github.io/guide/quick-start.html#installation) 165: 166: **Error: "Project folder must be an absolute path"** 167: - Use absolute paths: `/Users/me/project` not `./project` 168: - Use `pwd` to get current directory: `$(pwd)/project` 169: 170: **No duplicates found when you expect some:** 171: - Try lowering `--min-similarity` (e.g., 0.7) 172: - Try lowering `--min-lines` (e.g., 3) 173: - Check that the correct `--construct-type` is specified 174: - Verify the `--language` matches your codebase 175: 176: **Too many false positives:** 177: - Increase `--min-similarity` (e.g., 0.9) 178: - Increase `--min-lines` to focus on larger code blocks 1: set -euo pipefail 2: usage() { 3: cat <<'EOF' 4: Usage: 5: only) 14: --regex Treat --from as regex (default: literal) 15: --word Match token boundaries for --from (good for numbers) 16: --glob <pattern> Include only matching files (repeatable) 17: --context <n> Context lines for preview (default: 2) 18: --help Show this help 19: Examples: 20: scripts/replace-magic.sh --from 1000 --to TIME_MS.SECOND --word --glob '*.ts' sidequest api 21: paths+=("$1") 98: shift 99: ;; 100: esac 101: done 102: if [[ -z "$from" || -z "$to" ]]; then 103: echo "Both --from and --to are required." >&2 104: usage 105: exit 1 106: fi 107: if [[ ! 33: return base_url.split("//")[-1].split(".")[0].title() 34: def discover_json_schemas(self, directory: Path, exclude_patterns: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> List[Path]: 35: if exclude_patterns is f"{self.base_url} 64: def validate_entity_id(self, entity_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 65: warnings = [] 66: if not entity_id.startswith("https://"): 67: warnings.append("@id should use HTTPS all entities (objects with @type and @id) from Schema.org JSON. 82: Args: 83: data: JSON data structure 84: parent_path: Path in the data tree (for debugging) 85: Returns: 86: List of extracted entities 87: """ 88: entities = [] 89: if isinstance(data, dict): 90: # If this is an entity (has @type and @id) 91: if "@type" in data and "@id" in data: 92: entity = {"@id": data["@id"], "@type": 97: entity[key] = {"@id": value["@id"]} 98: elif isinstance(value, dict) and "@type" in value and "@id" in value: 99: nested_entities = self.extract_entities_from_schema(value, f"{parent_path}.{key}") 100: entities.extend(nested_entities) 101: entity[key] = {"@id": value["@id"]} 102: elif isinstance(value, list): 103: new_list = [] 104: for i, item in enumerate(value): 105: if isinstance(item, self.extract_entities_from_schema(item, f"{parent_path}.{key}[{i}]") 109: entities.extend(nested_entities) 110: new_list.append({"@id": item["@id"]}) 111: else: 112: new_list.append(item) 113: entity[key] = new_list 114: else: 115: entity[key] = value 116: entities.append(entity) 117: else: 118: for key, value in data.items(): 119: if key == "@context": 120: continue 121: if key == "@graph" continue 126: if isinstance(value, (dict, list)): 127: entities.extend(self.extract_entities_from_schema(value, f"{parent_path}.{key}")) 128: elif isinstance(data, list): 129: for i, item in merge_duplicate_entities(self, entities: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 134: """ 135: Merge entities with the same @id, combining their properties. 136: Args: 137: entities: List of entities to merge 138: Returns: 139: List of unique entities 140: """ 141: entity_map = {} 142: for entity in entities: 143: entity_id = entity["@id"] 144: if entity_id not in entity_map: 145: entity_map[entity_id] = entity 146: else: 147: existing = entity_map[entity_id] 148: if len(entity.keys()) > len(existing.keys()): 149: for key, value in existing.items(): 150: if key not in entity and key != "parent_path": 151: entity[key] = value 152: entity_map[entity_id] = entity 153: else: 154: for key, value in entity.items(): 155: if key not in existing and key != "parent_path": 156: existing[key] = value 157: return list(entity_map.values()) 158: def build_entity_graph(self, json_files: List[Path]) -> Dict[str, Any]: 159: all_entities = [] 160: console.log(f"

36: } catch (error) { 37: return { 38: path: filePath, 39: fullPath, 40: error: error.message, 41: isValid: false, 42: }; 43: } 44: } 45: async function fixFilePermissions(filePath) { 46: const fullPath = path.join(ROOT, filePath); `); 56: for (const result of invalid) { 57: if (result.error) { 58: console.log(` ❌ ${result.path}`); 59: console.log(` Error: ${result.error}
`); 60: } else if (result.isExecutable) { 61: console.log(` ⚠️ ${result.path}`); 62: console.log(` Mode: ${result.mode} (executable)`); 63: console.log(` Expected: 100644 (not executable)

") 281: in_summary = False 282: for line in lines[: SemanticVolumeDefaults.SUMMARY_PREVIEW_LIMIT]: 283: if "## Summary" in line or "## Executive Summary" in line: 284: in_summary = True 285: if in_summary: 286: out(line) 287: if line.startswith("##") and "Summary" not in line: 288: break 289: def generate_summary_report(project_folder: str, language: str, apply_fixes: bool = False): 290: print_section("PHASE 6: Generate Comprehensive Quality Report") 291: try: 292: enforcement_result = _run_enforcement(project_folder, language) 293: result = _generate_markdown_report(enforcement_result) 294: _print_report_summary(result) 295: if apply_fixes: 296: _apply_fixes(enforcement_result, language, project_folder=project_folder) 297: except Exception as e: 298: out(f"Exception during report generation: {e}") 299: import traceback 300: traceback.print_exc() 301: def _run_tsc_check(project_folder: str) -> bool: 302: """Run tsc --noEmit to verify no type errors after fixes. 303: Args: 304: project_folder: Path to the project folder 305: Returns: 306: True if tsc passes (or is not available), False if errors found 307: """ 308: tsconfig = Path(project_folder) / "tsconfig.json" 309: if not tsconfig.exists(): 310: return True 311: out("
Running tsc --noEmit to verify fixes...") 312: try: 313: result = subprocess.run( 314: ["npx", "tsc", "--noEmit"], 315: cwd=project_folder, 316: capture_output=True, 317: text=True, 318: timeout=120, 319: ) 320: if result.returncode == 0: 321: out("tsc --noEmit: PASSED (no type errors)") 322: return True 323: error_lines = result.stdout.strip().splitlines() if result.stdout else [] 324: error_count = sum(1 for line in error_lines if ": error TS" in line) 325: out(f"tsc --noEmit: FAILED ({error_count} type errors)") 326: # Show bounded error preview 327: for line in error_lines[: SemanticVolumeDefaults.DETAIL_RESULTS_LIMIT]: 328: if ": error TS" in line: 329: out(f" {line}") 330: if error_count > SemanticVolumeDefaults.DETAIL_RESULTS_LIMIT: 331: out(f" ... and {error_count - SemanticVolumeDefaults.DETAIL_RESULTS_LIMIT} more errors") 332: return False 333: except FileNotFoundError: 334: out("tsc not found, skipping type check") 335: return True 336: except subprocess.TimeoutExpired: 337: out("tsc --noEmit timed out after 120s, skipping") 338: return True 339: def _is_cli_entry_point(file_path: str) -> bool: 340: try: 341: content = Path(file_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8") 342: return "__name__" in content and "'__main__'" in content or '"__main__"' in content 343: except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError): 344: return False 345: _DESTRUCTIVE_RULES = {"no-print-production", "no-console-log", "no-system-out"} 346: def _filter_destructive_violations(violations: list[dict]) -> tuple[list[dict], int]: 347: filtered = [] 348: skipped = 0 349: cli_cache: dict[str, bool] = {} 350: for v in violations: 351: rule_id = v.get("rule_id", "") 352: file_path = v.get("file", "") 353: if rule_id in _DESTRUCTIVE_RULES: 354: if file_path not in cli_cache: 355: cli_cache[file_path] = _is_cli_entry_point(file_path) 356: if cli_cache[file_path]: 357: skipped += 1 358: continue 359: filtered.append(v) 360: return filtered, skipped 361: def _apply_fixes(enforcement_result: dict, language: str, project_folder: str = ""): 362: """Apply automatic standards fixes from enforcement violations.""" 363: print_section("PHASE 7: Apply Standards Fixes") 364: violations = enforcement_result.get("violations", []) 365: if not violations: 366: out("
No violations to fix.") 367: return 368: violations, skipped = _filter_destructive_violations(violations) 369: if skipped: 370: out(f"
Skipped {skipped} violations in CLI/test files (removal rules would delete intentional output)") 371: if not violations: 372: out("No remaining violations to fix after filtering.") 373: return 374: try: 375: # Dry run first 376: dry_result = apply_standards_fixes_tool( 377: violations=violations, 378: language=language, 379: fix_types=["safe"], 380: dry_run=True, 381: create_backup=True, 382: ) 383: summary = dry_result.get("summary", {}) 384: fixable = summary.get("total_violations", 0) 385: safe_count = sum(1 for r in dry_result.get("results", []) if r.get("fix_type") == "safe") 386: out(f"
Dry run: {safe_count} of {fixable} violations can be auto-fixed (safe fixes only)") 387: if safe_count == 0: 388: out("No auto-fixable violations found.") 389: return 390: # Apply fixes 391: fix_result = apply_standards_fixes_tool( 392: violations=violations, 393: language=language, 394: fix_types=["safe"], 395: dry_run=False, 396: create_backup=True, 397: ) 398: fix_summary = fix_result.get("summary", {}) 399: out(f"
Fixed: {fix_summary.get('fixes_successful', 0)} violations") 400: out(f"Failed: {fix_summary.get('fixes_failed', 0)}") 401: out(f"Files modified: {fix_summary.get('files_modified', 0)}") 402: backup_id = fix_result.get("backup_id") 403: if backup_id: 404: out(f"Backup ID: {backup_id}") 405: # Post-fix type check for TypeScript 406: if language == "typescript" and project_folder: 407: if not _run_tsc_check(project_folder): 408: out(f"
WARNING: Type errors detected after fixes. "o200k_base" 128: } 129: } 1: import json 2: import sys 3: import time 4: from pathlib import Path 5: def run_complexity(project_folder: str) -> dict: 6: from ast_grep_mcp.features.complexity.tools import analyze_complexity_tool 7: result = analyze_complexity_tool(project_folder=project_folder, language="python") 8: print("
=== COMPLEXITY ANALYSIS ===") 9: print(json.dumps(result["summary"], indent=2)) 10: funcs = result.get("functions", []) 11: print(f"Functions exceeding thresholds: {len(funcs)}") 12: # Response keys: name, file, lines, cyclomatic, cognitive, nesting_depth, length, exceeds 13: for f in sorted(funcs, key=lambda x: x.get("cognitive", 0), reverse=True)[:10]: 14: rel = f.get("file", "").split("src/")[-1] 15: print(f" {rel} {f['name']} cyc={f['cyclomatic']} cog={f['cognitive']} nest={f['nesting_depth']} len={f['length']}") 16: if len(funcs) > 10: 17: print(f" ... and {len(funcs) - 10} more") 18: return result 19: def run_smells(project_folder: str) -> dict: 20: from ast_grep_mcp.features.complexity.tools import detect_code_smells_tool 21: result = detect_code_smells_tool(project_folder=project_folder, language="python") 22: print("
=== CODE SMELLS ===") 23: summary = {k: v for k, v in result.items() if k != "smells"} 24: print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2)) 25: smells = result.get("smells", []) 26: for s in smells[:10]: 27: rel = s.get("file", "").split("src/")[-1] 28: print(f" [{s.get('severity', '?')}] {s.get('smell_type', '?')}: {rel}:{s.get('line', '')} {s.get('message', '')[:80]}") 29: if len(smells) > 10: 30: print(f" ... and {len(smells) - 10} more smells") 31: return result 32: def run_standards(project_folder: str) -> dict: 33: """Run standards enforcement.""" 34: from ast_grep_mcp.features.quality.tools import enforce_standards_tool 35: result = enforce_standards_tool(project_folder=project_folder, language="python", rule_set="all") 36: print("
=== STANDARDS ENFORCEMENT ===") 37: print(json.dumps(result["summary"], indent=2)) 38: violations = result.get("violations", []) 39: for v in violations[:10]: 40: rel = v["file"].split("src/")[-1] 41: print(f" [{v['severity']}] {v['rule_id']}: {rel}:{v['line']} {v['message'][:80]}") 42: if len(violations) > 10: 43: print(f" ... and {len(violations) - 10} more violations") 44: return result 45: def run_security(project_folder: str) -> dict: 46: from ast_grep_mcp.features.quality.tools import detect_security_issues_tool 47: result = detect_security_issues_tool(project_folder=project_folder, language="python") 48: print("
=== SECURITY SCAN ===") 49: print(json.dumps(result["summary"], indent=2)) 50: issues = result.get("issues", []) 51: for i in issues[:10]: 52: rel = i["file"].split("src/")[-1] 53: print(f" [{i['severity']}] {i['issue_type']}: {rel}:{i['line']} {i['title'][:80]}") 54: if len(issues) > 10: 55: print(f" ... and {len(issues) - 10} more issues") 56: return result 57: def run_orphans(project_folder: str) -> dict: 58: from ast_grep_mcp.features.quality.tools import detect_orphans_tool 59: result = detect_orphans_tool(project_folder=project_folder) 60: print("
=== ORPHAN DETECTION ===") 61: print(json.dumps(result["summary"], indent=2)) 62: for f in result.get("orphan_files", [])[:10]: 63: rel = f["file_path"].split("src/")[-1] 64: print(f" FILE: {rel} ({f.get('lines', '?')} lines) {f.get('status', '')}") 65: for f in result.get("orphan_functions", [])[:10]: 66: rel = f.get("file", "").split("src/")[-1] 67: print(f" FUNC: {rel}:{f.get('line', '')} {f.get('name', '')}") 68: return result 69: def run_duplication(project_folder: str) -> dict: 70: """Run duplication detection.""" 71: from ast_grep_mcp.features.deduplication.tools import find_duplication_tool 72: result = find_duplication_tool(project_folder=project_folder, language="python") 73: print("
=== DUPLICATION DETECTION ===") 74: summary = result.get("summary", {}) 75: print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2)) 76: groups = result.get("duplication_groups", []) 77: for g in groups[:10]: 78: print(f" Group {g.get('group_id', '?')}: sim={g.get('similarity_score', 0):.2f}") 79: for inst in g.get("instances", [])[:3]: 80: rel = inst.get("file", "").split("src/")[-1] 81: print(f" {rel} lines={inst.get('lines', '')}") 82: if len(groups) > 10: 83: print(f" ... and {len(groups) - 10} more groups") 84: return result 85: def run_benchmarks() -> dict: 86: """Run deduplication performance benchmarks.""" 87: from ast_grep_mcp.features.deduplication.tools import benchmark_deduplication_tool 88: result = benchmark_deduplication_tool(iterations=5) 89: print("
=== DEDUPLICATION BENCHMARKS ===") 90: summary = {k: v for k, v in result.items() if k != "results"} 91: print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2)) 92: for r in result.get("results", []): 93: print(f" {r['name']}: mean={r.get('mean_ms', 0):.2f}ms median={r.get('median_ms', 0):.2f}ms p95={r.get('p95_ms', 0):.2f}ms") 94: return result 95: def main() -> None: 96: project_folder = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "src") 97: print(f"Analyzing: {project_folder}") 98: start = time.time() 99: run_complexity(project_folder) 100: run_smells(project_folder) 101: run_standards(project_folder) 102: run_security(project_folder) 103: run_orphans(project_folder) 104: run_duplication(project_folder) 105: run_benchmarks() 106: elapsed = time.time() - start 107: print(f"
=== COMPLETE === ({elapsed:.1f}s)") 108: if __name__ == "__main__": 109: main() 1: set -euo pipefail 2: SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$0")" 3: ROOT="${1:?Usage: $SCRIPT_NAME <root_dir> <output_file> [include_logs_count]}" 4: OUTPUT_FILE="${2:?Usage: $SCRIPT_NAME <root_dir> <output_file> [include_logs_count]}" 5: INCLUDE_LOGS_COUNT="${3:-200}" 6: # Preflight: required commands. 7: for cmd in jq npx git; do 8: command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1 \ 9: || { echo "$SCRIPT_NAME: required command not found: $cmd" >&2; exit 1; } 10: done 11: ROOT="$(cd "$ROOT" 2>/dev/null && pwd)" \ 12: || { echo "ROOT does not exist or is not accessible: ${1}" >&2; exit 1; } 13: if ! git -C "$ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then 14: echo "ROOT is not a git repository: $ROOT" >&2 15: exit 1 16: fi 17: OUTPUT_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$OUTPUT_FILE")" 2>/dev/null && pwd)/$(basename "$OUTPUT_FILE")" \ 18: || { echo "Cannot resolve output file path: ${2}" >&2; exit 1; } 19: # Validate output directory exists. 20: OUTPUT_DIR="$(dirname "$OUTPUT_FILE")" 21: if [[ ! -d "$OUTPUT_DIR" ]]; then 22: echo "Output directory does not exist: $OUTPUT_DIR" >&2 23: exit 1 24: fi 25: INCLUDE_DIFFS="${REPOMIX_INCLUDE_DIFFS:-true}" 26: INCLUDE_LOGS="${REPOMIX_INCLUDE_LOGS:-true}" 27: SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS="${REPOMIX_SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS:-1000}" 28: TIMEOUT_SECONDS="${REPOMIX_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-120}" 29: REPOMIX_LOG_TAIL_LINES="${REPOMIX_LOG_TAIL_LINES:-20}" 30: if ! [[ "$INCLUDE_LOGS_COUNT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [[ "$INCLUDE_LOGS_COUNT" -lt 0 ]]; then 31: echo "include_logs_count must be a non-negative integer: $INCLUDE_LOGS_COUNT" >&2 32: exit 1 33: fi 34: if ! [[ "$SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [[ "$SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS" -lt 1 ]]; then 35: echo "REPOMIX_SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS must be a positive integer: $SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS" >&2 36: exit 1 37: fi 38: if [[ "$INCLUDE_DIFFS" != "true" && "$INCLUDE_DIFFS" != "false" ]]; then 39: echo "REPOMIX_INCLUDE_DIFFS must be true|false: $INCLUDE_DIFFS" >&2 40: exit 1 41: fi 42: if [[ "$INCLUDE_LOGS" != "true" && "$INCLUDE_LOGS" != "false" ]]; then 43: echo "REPOMIX_INCLUDE_LOGS must be true|false: $INCLUDE_LOGS" >&2 44: exit 1 45: fi 46: if [[ "$INCLUDE_LOGS_COUNT" -eq 0 && "$INCLUDE_LOGS" == "true" ]]; then 47: echo "Warning: INCLUDE_LOGS=true but include_logs_count=0; no logs will be included" >&2 48: fi 49: TMP_CONFIG="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/repomix-git-ranked.XXXXXX.json")" 50: TMP_FILES="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/repomix-git-ranked-files.XXXXXX")" 51: TMP_LOG="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/repomix-run.XXXXXX.log")" 52: trap 'rm -f "$TMP_CONFIG" "$TMP_FILES" "$TMP_LOG"' EXIT INT TERM 53: # Generated bundle patterns sourced from base repomix config. 54: BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_FILE="$ROOT/repomix.config.json" 55: if [[ -f "$BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_FILE" ]]; then 56: BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_JSON="$( 57: jq -c ' 58: [ 59: .ignore.customPatterns[]? 60: | select( 61: . == "docs/repomix/**" 62: or . == "**/repomix-output.*" 63: or . == "**/repo-compressed.*" 64: or . == "**/repomix.xml" 65: or . == "**/repo-compressed.xml" 66: ) 67: ] | unique 68: ' "$BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_FILE" 2>/dev/null 69: )" || { 70: echo "Warning: $BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_FILE exists but is not valid JSON; using empty ignore patterns" >&2 71: BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_JSON='[]' 72: } 73: else 74: BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_JSON='[]' 75: fi 76: # Generated artifacts that should never be re-ingested into ranked bundles. 77: # Override via REPOMIX_EXCLUDE_ARTIFACTS (newline-separated glob patterns). 78: # Patterns are intentionally interpreted as globs by case. 79: # Do not pass untrusted input in REPOMIX_EXCLUDE_ARTIFACTS. 80: EXCLUDE_ARTIFACTS="${REPOMIX_EXCLUDE_ARTIFACTS:-sidequest/docs/gitlog-sidequest.txt}" 81: is_generated_bundle_artifact() { 82: local rel_path="$1" 83: local pattern 84: while IFS= read -r pattern; do 85: [[ -z "$pattern" ]] && continue 86: # shellcheck disable=SC2254 87: case "$rel_path" in 88: $pattern) return 0 ;; 89: esac 90: done <<< "$EXCLUDE_ARTIFACTS" 91: return 1 92: } 93: # Build include list from files touched in the selected commit window. 94: # Use sort -u for deduplication (compatible with bash 3.x on macOS). 95: git -C "$ROOT" -c core.quotePath=false log --name-only --pretty=format: -n "$SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS" \ 96: | grep -v '^$' \ 97: | sort -u \ 98: | while IFS= read -r rel_path; do 99: if is_generated_bundle_artifact "$rel_path"; then 100: continue 101: fi 102: if [[ -f "$ROOT/$rel_path" ]]; then 103: printf '%s
' "$rel_path" 104: fi 105: done > "$TMP_FILES" 106: if [[ ! -s "$TMP_FILES" ]]; then 107: echo "No existing files found in the selected commit window." >&2 108: exit 1 109: fi 110: include_files_json="$(jq -R -s 'split("
") | map(select(length > 0))' "$TMP_FILES")" 111: jq -n \ 112: --argjson includeFiles "$include_files_json" \ 113: --argjson bundleIgnorePatterns "$BUNDLE_IGNORE_PATTERNS_JSON" \ 114: --argjson includeLogsCount "$INCLUDE_LOGS_COUNT" \ 115: --argjson sortByChangesMaxCommits "$SORT_BY_CHANGES_MAX_COMMITS" \ 116: --arg includeDiffs "$INCLUDE_DIFFS" \ 117: --arg includeLogs "$INCLUDE_LOGS" ' 118: { 119: "$schema": "https://repomix.com/schemas/latest/schema.json", 120: "output": { 121: "style": "xml", 122: "parsableStyle": true, 123: "removeComments": true, 124: "removeEmptyLines": true, 125: "fileSummary": false, 126: "directoryStructure": false, 127: "files": true, 128: "includeEmptyDirectories": false, 129: "git": { 130: "sortByChanges": true, 131: "sortByChangesMaxCommits": $sortByChangesMaxCommits, 132: "includeDiffs": ($includeDiffs == "true"), 133: "includeLogs": ($includeLogs == "true"), 134: "includeLogsCount": $includeLogsCount 135: } 136: }, 137: "ignore": { 138: "useGitignore": true, 139: "useDotIgnore": true, 140: "useDefaultPatterns": true, 141: "customPatterns": $bundleIgnorePatterns 142: }, 143: "include": $includeFiles 144: }' > "$TMP_CONFIG" 145: REPOMIX_EXIT=0 146: FORCE_COLOR=0 NO_COLOR=1 timeout "$TIMEOUT_SECONDS" \ 147: npx repomix "$ROOT" -c "$TMP_CONFIG" -o "$OUTPUT_FILE" >"$TMP_LOG" 2>&1 \ 148: || REPOMIX_EXIT=$? 149: if [[ $REPOMIX_EXIT -ne 0 ]]; then 150: echo "repomix failed (exit $REPOMIX_EXIT). Last output:" >&2 151: tail -"$REPOMIX_LOG_TAIL_LINES" "$TMP_LOG" >&2 152: exit 1 153: fi
repomix.instruction: Modular MCP server with ast-grep structural code search, Schema.org tools, refactoring, deduplication, quality, documentation generation, and semantic code condensation.
